query: A researcher claims the average resting heart rate for adults is 72 bpm. A sample recorded [68,74,71,75,69,73,72,70] bpm. Is there statistical evidence to dispute the claim at p<0.05?
A quality-control team recorded widget diameters (mm): [10.1,10.3,10.0,9.9,10.2,14.8,10.1]. Flag any anomalous measurements.
Before running a parametric test, verify that exam scores [72,75,74,78,73,76,77,74,75,73] meet the normality assumption.
Estimate the 99% confidence interval for mean blood pressure from the sample [120,118,125,122,119,123,121].
A teacher wants to standardise quiz scores across classes for fair comparison. Standardise: [55, 70, 85, 90, 60].
Monthly ad spend ($k) was [1,2,3,4,5] and corresponding sales (units) were [120,150,180,200,230]. Build a model to predict sales from spend.
A sensor stream has noise: [22,25,23,28,30,27,31,29,33,35]. Smooth the signal with a 4-period window.
Study hours logged by students were [2,3,5,6,7] and their test scores were [55,65,80,88,92]. Quantify how the two variables move together.
A salary dataset shows most employees earn around $50k but a few executives earn over $500k. The values are [48000,51000,49000,52000,50000,53000,480000,520000]. Measure the asymmetry.
An analyst needs a full numerical summary of daily website visits for the past two weeks: [1200,1350,980,1100,1420,1300,900,1250,1180,1400,1050,1320,970,1230].
Risk analysts want to know if returns distribution [0.02,-0.01,0.03,0.015,-0.005,0.025,0.01,-0.02,0.018,0.012] has heavy tails compared to a normal distribution.
A logistics team tracks package delivery times (hours): [24,18,36,22,19,42,21,28,17,35,20,30]. Provide the quartile breakdown to understand spread.
